problem.title: Three quantified risks most CIOs still under-estimate.
problem.subtitle: As long as IT decommissioning stays a year-end item handled by four separate vendors, you accumulate regulatory, financial and carbon debt, and you discover it during the audit.

items.title: GDPR fines up to 4% of global revenue. NIS2 and DORA add a binding chain-of-custody obligation.
items.body: GDPR art. 83 caps fines at €20m or 4% of consolidated global revenue. Since 17 January 2025, DORA (EU Reg. 2022/2554, art. 28-30) requires every ITAD vendor of a financial entity to be listed on the TPP register and audited annually. NIS2 (transposed 18 October 2024) extends liability to executives.
items.source: Sources: CNIL, ACPR Notice 2024-N-04, ENISA NIS2 Toolkit

items.title: €412 of residual value left on the table per IT seat, pure leakage.
items.body: On the EMEA refurb market, a 3-year-old business laptop is worth €280 to €540 depending on spec and grade (GTC Asset Intelligence estimator, n=1,487 graded assets 2024-2025). With no market valuation, most mid-caps sell off as scrap metal, at €12 a tonne.
items.source: Sources: GTC Asset Intelligence 2025, Recommerce Market Index Q4 2024

items.title: A new laptop emits 156 kgCO₂e at manufacturing. Refurbished, only 6.2 kgCO₂e.
items.body: A 25× factor on the most emissive phase of the lifecycle. Across an 1,800-seat refresh, the difference is 270 tCO₂e, equivalent to 1.5 million km in a diesel Renault Clio.
items.source: Sources: ADEME Base Empreinte® v23 (laptop, EF 156 kgCO₂e), Boavizta API v1.4 (refurb 6.2 kgCO₂e)

hero.title: The platform that turns your IT end-of-life into auditable evidence.
hero.subtitle: One single chain, from inventory to certificate. Your auditors receive in two clicks what your teams used to spend weeks rebuilding.
hero.floatQuote: We connected the platform to our CMDB on a Tuesday. By Friday, the ACPR auditor had pulled their own dossier.

promise.title: You decide. The platform proves it.
promise.body1: Your IT, security and finance teams spend their days rebuilding evidence. Serial numbers retrieved by hand, erasure certificates lost in an email, inventory gaps debated in meetings. All while the regulatory deadline keeps moving.
promise.body2: Our platform does not replace your field providers: it orchestrates them. Every asset enters through a single door, follows a coded path and exits as a dossier your auditors recognise. No pivot tables, no re-keying, no blind spots.

chapters.title: Your existing sources become a single source of truth.
chapters.body: Your asset registries, your CMDB exports, your MDM feeds, your fleet management spreadsheets: the platform ingests and reconciles them. Every gap is flagged, every duplicate is resolved, every ghost asset is exposed. Your IT leadership regains control of a fleet they thought they knew.
chapters.photoAlt: GreenTechCycle technician scanning an IT asset in a server room with end-to-end traceability
bullets: Native connectors for ServiceNow, GLPI, Intune, JAMF, EasyVista
Bulk imports, web interface or barcode field scanning
Automatic detection of inconsistencies and orphan assets

chapters.title: Each asset reveals its risk, value and impact.
chapters.body: When an asset arrives, the platform asks three questions and answers them on its own. What residual cyber risk remains on this disk? What residual value can the secondary market recover? What carbon avoidance comes from extending its life? The resulting view serves IT, the CISO and the sustainability lead at once.
chapters.photoAlt: Security leadership reviewing the asset-by-asset risk dashboard
chapters.proofDetail: Risk, value and carbon score computed for every eligible asset
bullets: Residual vulnerability score per disk and per OS
Secondary-market valuation refreshed every week
Carbon footprint computed using ADEME Base Empreinte v23 factors

chapters.title: The platform recommends. Your policies decide.
chapters.body: Refurbish, resell, donate, recycle or destroy: the platform suggests the best route given your internal policies, data sensitivity, sovereignty commitments, regulatory calendar, reuse target. You approve in one click. The decision rationale is logged, opposable and exportable.
chapters.photoAlt: IT leadership ruling on the end-of-life of an asset fleet on the platform
chapters.proofDetail: Versus 32% observed in standard professional WEEE short-loop circuits
bullets: Rule engine configurable by sensitivity, sector and site
Four-eyes validation for high-stake decisions
Decision rationale logged for every outgoing asset

chapters.title: From pickup to certificate, nothing slips away.
chapters.body: The numbered seal is applied at your premises, the transport is tracked, the erasure is filmed, the certificate is eIDAS-signed and timestamped. If an auditor asks for the chain of custody on a specific asset, you produce it in under a minute. Not three weeks, not via six different contacts.
chapters.photoAlt: Electronic signature of an erasure certificate after quality control
chapters.proofDetail: Contractual commitment after reception at the processing floor
bullets: NIST 800-88 rev. 1 erasure (Clear, Purge, Destroy) by sensitivity
Time-stamped video surveillance of the processing floor
eIDAS-signed certificates with cryptographic seal kept ten years

chapters.title: One dossier per stakeholder, ready without rework.
chapters.body: IT receives its gap dashboard, the CISO their evidence matrix, finance their accounting extract, sustainability their ESRS E5 chapter. Every format matches what your auditors and statutory accountants expect. The platform does not produce paper: it ships actionable dossiers.

Accounting extracts compatible with SAP, Sage and Cegid
Read-only auditor access delivered within 24 hours

faq.title: What IT leaders ask before signing.

items.q: How long to connect the platform to our asset registry?
items.a: Two to five days for the native connectors (ServiceNow, GLPI, Intune, JAMF). For an in-house registry, our integration team ships a custom connector in two to three weeks.

items.q: Where is our data hosted and who can access it?
items.a: Hosted on OVHcloud SecNumCloud, in mainland France. No subprocessor outside the European Union. AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit. Internal access is logged and subject to four-eyes for sensitive operations.

items.q: Which erasure level applies to which sensitivity?
items.a: Three NIST 800-88 rev. 1 levels (Clear, Purge, Destroy) based on your data classification. SSDs and NVMe drives also follow IEEE 2883. Each operation produces an eIDAS-signed certificate kept ten years.

items.q: How do auditors access the evidence on the day?
items.a: Your auditors receive read-only, perimeter-scoped access delivered within 24 hours. They can browse, filter and export without depending on your internal teams. Every access is logged and revocable in one click.

manifesto.title: Bring IT end-of-life into the real corporate books.
manifesto.body1: For twenty years, IT end-of-life was treated as a chore. You signed a slip, handed it to a carrier, hoped no hard drive ever resurfaced. That era is ending. Regulators demand evidence, statutory auditors review the entries, and executive leaders discover this line item really weighs on the group's carbon balance.
manifesto.body2: We founded GreenTechCycle because nobody, in 2021, offered these leaders a complete, opposable, readable chain. Not a recycler inventing a software layer, not a software vendor without a field operation. A real chain, from the seal applied at your premises to the certificate kept for ten years, run by teams who own their results.
manifesto.body3: Our 2030 ambition is plain: 80% of the French professional IT fleet must run through a short, refurbished, traceable and auditable circuit. Not as an activist option, as a management standard.

founder.title: A decision made in a bank basement in 2019.
founder.quote: I watched two thousand hard drives shredded that day with not a single certificate. The invoice was paid, the risk was real and nobody in the room could prove the data would never reappear. GreenTechCycle was born from that anger.
founder.name: Mahdi A.
founder.bio: Engineering graduate, fifteen years of experience including eight as a CISO in retail banking. CISSP and ISO 27001 Lead Auditor. Active member of Boavizta and the French Institut du Numérique Responsable. GreenTechCycle was founded in Lyon in 2021.

convictions.title: No client data ever leaves European soil.
convictions.body: We made that choice on day one, before it was a contractual requirement. Our servers run on OVHcloud SecNumCloud, our subprocessors are European, our backups are encrypted and stored in France. When a large enterprise asks where their data lives, the answer fits in one sentence and one architecture diagram.
convictions.photoAlt: Sovereign hosting room in France used by GreenTechCycle
bullets: Hosted on OVHcloud SecNumCloud, mainland France
Subprocessors located exclusively within the European Union
No client data transfer to extra-European jurisdictions

convictions.title: Every figure we publish is sourced. Every operation is filmed.
convictions.body: We publish our scope 1+2+3 carbon balance every year, audited by an independent firm. We do not recycle marketing claims: ADEME, Boavizta, CIGREF, INR are our only sources. Our processing floors are under time-stamped video surveillance, our certificates are eIDAS-signed and kept for ten years. If you cannot reconstruct an operation, we did it wrong.

bullets: Scope 1+2+3 carbon balance published yearly and externally audited
Time-stamped video surveillance of every processing floor
eIDAS-signed certificates, cryptographic hash, decade-long archive

convictions.title: Twenty-seven percent of our technical staff comes from inclusion programmes.
convictions.body: Sorting a hard drive, refurbishing a laptop, applying a numbered seal: those skills are not learned on the engineering school benches. We train operators recruited through inclusion-through-economic-activity structures, in partnership with Envie, Ateliers du Bocage and Emmaüs Connect. Our field teams are both more stable and more accurate than the industry average.

We mean extending the use phase of equipment, because that is where 78% of an IT asset's footprint is decided. Every internal decision (hiring, purchasing, travel) is assessed against its measurable carbon impact. We apply to ourselves what we recommend to our clients.

hero.title: Regulations
hero.subtitle: Understanding the obligations that impact your ITAD

timeline.name: CSRD
timeline.description: Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive : Mandatory extra-financial reporting including electronic waste management and circular economy.
timeline.itadImpact: Mandatory traceability of end-of-life IT assets, scope 3 emissions reporting for hardware.
timeline.sanctions: Financial penalties and executive liability for non-compliance.

timeline.name: NIS2
timeline.description: Network and Information Security Directive : Extended scope to mid-size companies with strengthened security obligations.
timeline.itadImpact: Mandatory certified erasure for essential and important entities. Data-related incident notification.
timeline.sanctions: Up to €10M or 2% of global turnover for essential entities.

timeline.name: DORA
timeline.description: Digital Operational Resilience Act : Digital operational resilience for the financial sector.
timeline.itadImpact: Risk management for ITAD providers. Regular audits and tests of destruction processes.
timeline.sanctions: Administrative sanctions up to 1% of average daily turnover.

timeline.name: AI Act
timeline.description: European AI Regulation : Framework for AI systems based on risk levels.
timeline.itadImpact: Lifecycle management of AI systems embedded in IT hardware. Training data traceability.
timeline.sanctions: Up to €35M or 7% of global turnover for the most serious violations.

weight.title: The real weight of enterprise digital
weight.lead: IT, sustainability and procurement leaders systematically underestimate IT asset emissions because most of them are hidden upstream in the value chain (Scope 3 category 1, purchased goods and services). Three essential orders of magnitude.

stats.title: Manufacturing share of an IT device's footprint
stats.desc: Across a laptop's full life cycle, 78% of emissions are released before first use. Extending lifetime through refurbishment is the most effective IT decarbonization lever.

stats.title: Emissions avoided per refurbished laptop
stats.desc: Difference between new laptop manufacturing (169 kgCO₂e) and refurbishment of an existing laptop (17 kgCO₂e). Net 152 kgCO₂e avoided per device, excluding usage.
stats.source: ADEME Base Empreinte v23, datasheet IT-PORTABLE-2024

stats.title: Annual growth of digital emissions in France
stats.desc: Without circularity policies, tertiary digital emissions could triple by 2050. CSRD ESRS E5 makes circularity an opposable reporting requirement.

analogy.title: To set the scale
analogy.desc: A 10,000-device fleet renewed every 4 years emits ~423 tCO₂e/year for manufacturing alone in a buy-new mode. That's the equivalent of 2.2 million km in an average combustion car (ADEME, 0.193 kgCO₂e/km). Switching to GTC refurbishment cuts that line item below 50 tCO₂e/year, freeing 9 million car-km equivalents.

methodology.title: Our measurement methodology
methodology.lead: We apply a multi-framework methodology aligned with audit-grade standards. Every kgCO₂e we publish is traceable to a documented emission factor and an explicit system boundary.
methodology.scopeIntro: In line with the GHG Protocol Corporate Standard and Scope 3 Standard, GTC measures and reports the Scope 3 categories relevant to client IT assets.

scopes.title: Purchased goods & services
scopes.desc: Upstream emissions from manufacturing IT assets (laptops, desktops, displays, servers, mobiles). Per-asset calculation using ADEME / Boavizta emission factors. Hybrid spend-based + activity-based method.

scopes.title: Use of sold products
scopes.desc: For leased or as-a-service fleets, emissions from energy consumption during the use phase. RTE 2024 grid mix (52 gCO₂/kWh), prorated to actual operating hours.

scopes.title: End-of-life treatment
scopes.desc: Emissions from transport, depollution, dismantling and material recovery. GTC delivers the full cradle-to-grave perimeter, including avoided emissions (-) from reuse vs. virgin material production.

frameworks.name: GHG Protocol Corporate Standard + Scope 3 Standard
frameworks.desc: WRI/WBCSD, 2015 revision. International carbon accounting framework.

frameworks.name: ADEME Bilan Carbone® v8.7
frameworks.desc: Official French method, integrating Base Empreinte v23 emission factors (May 2024 release).

frameworks.name: ISO 14064-1:2018
frameworks.desc: Specification for the quantification and reporting of GHG emissions and removals at organization level.

frameworks.name: ISO 14067:2018
frameworks.desc: Carbon footprint of products, quantification requirements (product LCA).

frameworks.name: Boavizta API v1.4
frameworks.desc: Open methodology for the environmental assessment of digital equipment. Natively integrated into our calculations.

frameworks.name: NEGAOCTET
frameworks.desc: Reference framework for the environmental assessment of digital services (ADEME / ARCEP).

frameworks.name: INR Digital GHG Reference 2022
frameworks.desc: French Sustainable Digital Institute, sector measurement methodology.

methodology.boundaryDesc: Our perimeter covers the five LCA stages: raw material extraction (cradle), manufacturing & assembly, distribution, use, end-of-life (grave). Circular allocation follows the Boavizta method: a refurbished asset is not re-counted under Scope 3.1 for the second user (double-counting avoidance validated by ISO 14067 §7.4).

indicators.label: GHG emissions
indicators.desc: Global warming potential 100 yr, IPCC AR6

indicators.label: Water withdrawn
indicators.desc: Life-cycle water footprint, Water Scarcity Footprint method

indicators.label: Abiotic resources
indicators.desc: Mineral and fossil resource depletion, CML 2002 method

indicators.label: WEEE generated / avoided
indicators.desc: Tonnage of electrical and electronic waste avoided through reuse

calculator.title: IT carbon ROI calculator
calculator.subtitle: Estimated emissions avoided through GTC circularity, based on ADEME 2024 and Boavizta v1.4 emission factors. Four device types, configurable renewal rate.
calculator.scenarioA: Scenario A : Buy new every cycle
calculator.scenarioB: Scenario B : GTC refurbishment + lifetime extension
calculator.ctaResults: Receive the detailed report by e-mail
calculator.ctaResultsHint: Enter your business e-mail, we'll send a personalized PDF report (methodology + breakdown + recommendations) within 24h.
calculator.emailSuccess: Request received. Our sustainability team will contact you within 24 business hours with the personalized report.
calculator.methodBody: Avoided emissions = Σ (devices × renewal rate × (EF_new − EF_refurbished)). ADEME Base Empreinte v23 emission factors (May 2024): new laptop 169 kgCO₂e manufacturing + 25 kgCO₂e/yr usage; refurbished laptop 17 kgCO₂e refurbishment. New desktop 265 + 50/yr; refurbished ~30. New 1U server 1,500 + 1,200/yr; refurbished ~150. New smartphone 57 + 7/yr; refurbished ~6. Car-km equivalent: 0.193 kgCO₂e/km (ADEME 2024 average combustion car). Assumptions documented in /docs/carbon-methodology-sources.md.
calculator.disclaimer: Indicative estimate. Final calculations integrating your energy mix, actual usage durations and activity-data quality are produced under an ISO 14064-1 auditable assessment.

comparison.title: New vs. refurbished, verified data
comparison.lead: Six device categories, five environmental indicators. All values come from ADEME Base Empreinte v23, Boavizta Datavizta v1.4 and the HOP 2023 study on digital device durability.
comparison.footnoteBody: New EFs include extraction + manufacturing + transport + packaging. Refurbished EFs include collection, diagnostics, secure erasure (NIST 800-88), reconditioning, packaging and redistribution. The use phase is calculated separately (cat. 3.11) using the RTE 2024 grid mix (52 gCO₂/kWh). Water and WEEE volumes come from Boavizta API v1.4 (Datavizta method) and the HOP 2023 study "The environmental cost of a smartphone", extrapolated to other categories by mass-proportionality.
sources: HOP : Halte à l'Obsolescence Programmée 2023, The Environmental Cost of a Smartphone
INR Digital GHG Reference 2022, French Sustainable Digital Institute

hero.title: Frequently Asked Questions
hero.subtitle: Answers to the most common questions, organized by role

dsi.q: What erasure standards do you use?
dsi.a: We apply NIST 800-88 rev.1 (Clear, Purge, Destroy) and DoD 5220.22-M standards. Each operation generates an individual certificate with SHA-256 hash.

dsi.q: How do you guarantee chain of custody?
dsi.a: Every asset is tracked end-to-end: handover report, numbered seals, GPS transport, video-monitored processing and blockchain recording.

dsi.q: Is the platform compatible with our ITSM?
dsi.a: Yes, we offer native integrations with ServiceNow, GLPI, Intune and JAMF. Our REST API also enables custom integrations.
All data is hosted in France at OVHcloud (SecNumCloud qualification). AES-256 encryption at rest and TLS 1.3 in transit.
We guarantee 99.9% uptime SLA with 24/7 priority support for critical incidents.

dsi.q: How do you handle equipment under manufacturer warranty?
dsi.a: We systematically check warranty status and propose manufacturer return when relevant, before any ITAD processing.

dsi.q: Can you operate across multiple sites?
dsi.a: Yes, we operate across all of France and in 12 European countries. The platform natively supports multi-site management.

dsi.q: How does the platform RBAC work?
dsi.a: 5 predefined roles (Admin, Manager, Operator, Auditor, Reader) with 4-eyes principle for sensitive operations. Customization available.

rse.q: How do you calculate the carbon footprint?
rse.a: Via Boavizta API and ADEME emission factors. We cover scopes 1 (transport), 2 (energy) and 3 (emissions avoided through reuse).
Yes, our reports comply with ESRS standards, particularly E5 (circular economy). Automatic export in required formats.

rse.q: What is the average reuse rate?
rse.a: On average, 72% of collected equipment is refurbished and reused. The remainder is recycled through certified channels.
Yes, we work with a network of partner charities. Refurbished equipment can be directed to donations with tax receipts.

rse.q: How do you measure avoided emissions?
rse.a: By comparing the carbon impact of reuse vs manufacturing new equipment, using certified Boavizta methodology.

rse.q: Do you provide an annual impact report?
rse.a: Yes, each client receives a detailed annual impact report with carbon metrics, reuse rates, and SDG contributions.

daf.a: Per-asset pricing with volume discounts. No setup fees or hidden subscriptions. Custom quote within 48h.

daf.q: Is there a buyback value for equipment?
daf.a: Yes, refurbishable equipment generates a buyback value deducted from the invoice. Average 15-30% of residual value.
Detailed monthly billing by site and operation type. Accounting export compatible with SAP, Sage and Cegid.
Our clients see an average 200% ROI over 12 months through asset recovery and operational cost reduction.
Flexible 12 to 36-month contracts with exit clause. Option to start with a 3-month no-commitment pilot.

compliance.a: Yes, as a data processor under GDPR. DPA available, up-to-date processing register, designated DPO and supporting certifications.

compliance.q: How do you prove data destruction?
compliance.a: Individual erasure certificate per asset with SHA-256 hash, eIDAS timestamping and blockchain recording for irrefutable proof.
Yes, we facilitate audits: read-only platform access for auditors, pre-formatted reports and dedicated support.
Our certified erasure process meets NIS2 requirements for essential and important entities. Compliance documentation available.

compliance.q: How do you handle data transfers outside the EU?
compliance.a: No transfers outside the EU. French hosting, exclusively European subcontractors, GDPR compliance contractually guaranteed.

hero.title: Client use cases
hero.subtitle: 8 sector scenarios delivered in 2024-2025, each with hard numbers: tCO₂e avoided, € recovered, audit days saved, % compliance reached.

cases.title: Windows 11 migration and NIS2 compliance on 2,400 trading-floor workstations
cases.context: Tier-1 European bank, fleet of 2,400 trading-floor workstations to migrate to Windows 11 by 14 October 2025 (W10 end of support). Banking supervisor audit scheduled for March 2026, NIS2 in force since 18 October 2024, DORA in force since 17 January 2025. No formalised chain of custody until then, paper erasure certificates scattered.
cases.problem: The CISO must prove to the regulator that every decommissioned asset has been erased to NIST 800-88, transported under sealed chain of custody, destroyed or refurbished by a vendor listed in the DORA TPP register. Without this proof, supervisor sanction up to 1% of consolidated revenue.
cases.stakeholders: CISO, Workplace CIO, DPO, DORA Compliance, Operational risk director, Procurement
cases.solution: IT Fleet module connected to ServiceNow CMDB; NIST 800-88 Purge erasure with 4-eyes; eIDAS-sealed chain of custody and qualified RGS** timestamping; pre-configured DORA art. 28 + NIS2 + supervisor matrix; GTC Forensic Audit Pro Service for pre-audit; signed PDF/XBRL export to the supervisor.
cases.results: 2,400 workstations processed in 11 weeks (vs initial 6-month plan) · €187k residual value recovered · 312 tCO₂e avoided (Boavizta v1.4) · supervisor pre-audit cleared in 4 days instead of 3 weeks · 100% of certificates eIDAS-signed within 24 h.
cases.testimonialQuote: The eIDAS proof closed every supervisor question. For the first time, our CISO sleeps easy on a DORA audit.
cases.testimonialRole: IT Security Director, Tier-1 European bank (CAC40)

cases.title: Refurbishment and erasure of 800 medical-imaging drives without disrupting care
cases.context: Regional public teaching hospital, 9,000 beds, end-of-life medical imaging fleet (MRI, CT, ultrasound). 800 HDDs and SSDs hold patient DICOM imaging subject to 20-year retention and certified destruction. Health-data-hosting certification mandates physical destruction for any HDS-classified medium.
cases.problem: Continuity of care prohibits any examination interruption. Drives must be extracted, removed from the HDS perimeter, destroyed per national health-data agency standard, with chain of custody documented for a data protection authority audit. The DPO must sign a destruction protocol per asset without halting any operating block.
cases.stakeholders: Biomedical CIO, DPO, Quality & risk director, CISO, Sterilisation pharmacists, Regional health agency

items.q: How do you calculate the ROI of an ITAD mission?
items.a: We calculate ROI across 3 components: (1) residual value recovered (secondary market resale), (2) avoided costs (logistics, storage, compliance), (3) valorisable carbon credits. Every element is documented, sourced and presented in an ROI report delivered at D+30.

items.q: How long does a typical project take?
items.a: From 4 days (compliance flash audit) to 12 weeks (multi-year multi-site programme). Most large enterprise missions run 4 to 8 weeks, with a first interim report at D+10.

items.q: Does my data leave the EU?
items.a: No. 100% of processing is done on French soil across our 4 certified sites (Lyon, Paris, Bordeaux, Lille). No non-EU subcontracting. Full chain of custody documented from A to Z, available within 48h.

items.q: What SLA commitments do you offer?
items.a: Standard SLAs: onboarding within 72h, first report within 10 business days, erasure certificates within 48h post-intervention. Enhanced SLAs (24h) are available for critical DORA/NIS2 missions.

items.q: Do you work on classified sites?
items.a: Yes, under the appropriate clearance protocols. Our teams hold the necessary authorisations to intervene on classified Defence sites and critical national infrastructure operators (OIV). Protocol available on request.

items.q: How do I certify my waste disposal to my auditors?

editorialIntro.headline: Every IT transformation hides a strategic decision.
editorialIntro.text: Here are 8 times GreenTechCycle turned a regulatory constraint into a competitive advantage, for CIOs, CISOs, CFOs and sustainability directors who had better things to do than manage end-of-life IT.

editorialBodies.banque-cac40: In 2023, a tier-1 European bank faced the inevitable: 12,000 Windows 10 devices had to migrate before end of support, under the dual constraints of NIS2 and DORA. Every device leaving production represented a documentary risk, a potential attack surface for the next ACPR inspection. GreenTechCycle integrated the ITAD chain into the core of the migration plan, turning regulatory pressure into measurable advantage: €638k recovered, 1,850 tCO2e avoided.
editorialBodies.chu-sante: A 9,000-bed university hospital had accumulated a fleet of 4,200 end-of-life devices over five years, carrying patient data subject to medical confidentiality and the strictest GDPR requirements. The IT department had neither budget for new equipment nor tolerance for a data breach. GreenTechCycle deployed a NIST 800-88 certified erasure protocol within 5 working days, giving each machine a second life through a local supply chain. The DPO signed the report without reservation.
editorialBodies.industriel-csrd: A CAC60 industrial group needed to publish its first CSRD ESRS E5 report for fiscal year 2025, with Big 4 auditors demanding quantified evidence and irreproachable scope 3 traceability. Across 12 sites in 5 countries, OT equipment represented both a risk (industrial intellectual property) and an opportunity: unprovisioned residual value. GreenTechCycle delivered the CSRD report D-90 before year-end, validated without reservation on the first iteration.
editorialBodies.ministere-collectivite: A large municipality with 2,500 FTEs navigated a triple constraint: public procurement code requiring environmental criteria ≥5%, GDPR obligations on administrative data, and political demands for exemplary responsible purchasing. GreenTechCycle designed the first ITAD master plan integrating an approved ESS social clause, 100% of equipment resold through French supply chains, zero data processed outside national territory.
editorialBodies.retail-wakibox: Managing IT waste from 350 stores scattered across the country is both a logistical challenge and a regulatory obligation. Before WakiBox, each point of sale managed end-of-life equipment informally, making any WEEE audit impossible. GreenTechCycle industrialised collection with its smart IoT units, turning chaos into a traceable process, with a positive ROI from the sixth month and €89k in annual logistics savings.
editorialBodies.energie-dora: When the ACPR announces a DORA inspection, the countdown begins. Three weeks: that's the typical timeframe to produce a full chain of custody for a hybrid IT/OT fleet at a CAC40 utility. GreenTechCycle did it in 4 days, not by magic, but by method: battle-tested flash audit procedure, ACPR-ready templates, and a GTC expert on-site from D-1 to D+4 alongside the CISO. Zero documentary discrepancy. Zero penalty.
editorialBodies.telco-datacenter: Three data centres to decommission in 60 days. 2,800 servers. Telecom operator customer data under ANSSI requirements. And an IT director who had provisioned €400k in residual value, three times less than what GreenTechCycle would actually recover. The difference is international secondary market expertise: a network of qualified European buyers, real-time market pricing, and on-time execution.
editorialBodies.universite-ess: 4,500 teaching devices to decommission before the start of the academic year, with student data to erase and a public budget that didn't allow for destruction. GreenTechCycle organised in 10 days the certified NIST 800-88 erasure, grading into 3 categories, and donation to 12 ESS partner associations, turning a budget constraint into a concrete circular economy initiative. 520 tCO2e avoided. 4,500 computers given to students who needed them.

problem.title: Why traditional collection bins are no longer enough.
problem.body: Corepile, Screlec, Batribox or Élise deliver a useful service: they remove waste. But they measure nothing actionable. No weighing per stream, no real-time feedback, no CSRD mapping, and a quarterly aggregate report that helps neither the CFO, the sustainability lead nor the IT director justify anything. Your employees throw items away blindly, your auditors ask for proof, and no one holds reliable data to reconcile the two worlds.
problem.bodySecond: Waki Box flips the relationship: the bin produces the proof, not you. Weighing sensors, lithium-battery detection, stream-level traceability, a connected platform, ESRS E5 exports ready to drop into the sustainability report. Compliance becomes passive and measured, without adding cognitive load on the office manager.

flows.title: What the bin takes, what it doesn't.
flows.intro: Waki Box is designed for the diffuse small WEEE of office environments: what your employees empty from their drawers without knowing where to put it. For anything that holds data (laptops, servers, business smartphones) the bin is the wrong tool: that is where our distinct ITAD offer takes over with NIST 800-88 certified erasure.
flows.acceptedTitle: Streams accepted in the Waki Box
accepted: Portable Li-ion batteries (phones, keyboards, mice, connected toys)
USB, HDMI, Ethernet cables, power cords
LED bulbs, neon tubes, compact fluorescents
Accessories: wired headsets, earphones, remote controls
Office printer ink and toner cartridges
flows.excludedTitle: Excluded streams · handled by the GreenTechCycle ITAD offer
excluded: Active network gear (switches, firewalls, access points)
Storage media (hard drives, SSDs, tapes, USB sticks)

faq.title: Everything you want to know before you sign.

items.q: What actually happens on collection day?
items.a: Our approved hauler arrives in the agreed slot, weighs each stream in front of you, scans the labels, signs the waste tracking slip and leaves. The pickup takes 10 to 25 minutes depending on volume. Everything is updated on your platform within the hour.

items.q: How do the built-in sensors work?
items.a: Three sensors: per-compartment weighing, thermal probe, electrolyte gas detection. A lithium cell that is overheating or swelling triggers an email alert to your office manager and an automated call to our safety on-call line.

items.q: Which data is fed to the platform?
items.a: Weight per stream per day, fill rate, safety events, CO₂ equivalent avoided, recovered material value, ESRS E5 mapping per disclosure. Every dataset is exportable in CSV, Excel and CSRD XBRL.

items.q: What actually happens to the waste after pickup?
items.a: Sorting on our approved site, batch-level traceability, routing to the WEEE EPR scheme by stream. No incineration without recovery, no shipping outside the European Union for hazardous streams. End-of-life certificates are issued within 21 days.

items.q: How do I cancel if the service does not fit?
items.a: 30-day no-fee trial. Beyond that, 60 days notice on the Essential and Comfort plans, 90 days on Premium. No exit fee, simple bin retrieval by our hauler.

items.q: Is the service really CSRD and ESRS E5 ready?
items.a: Yes. The mapping is documented line by line against E5-1 (incoming resources), E5-2 (outflows), E5-3 (policies), E5-4 (actions), E5-5 (targets) and E5-6 (financial amounts). Reviewed by two Big 4 firms.

items.q: How long does Waki Box take to deploy on site?
items.a: Essential plan: 5 working days from signature to first pickup. Comfort plan: 10 days for 3 units. Premium multi-site plan: 4 to 6 weeks depending on the number of sites and information-system integration.

items.q: Can we test before committing?
items.a: Yes. The pilot programme offers 6 months at the founders' rate. Outside the pilot, we run a 30-minute video demo and arrange a reference visit in Lyon or Paris.

finalQuote.text: WEEE compliance is not a hidden month-end cost. Properly instrumented, it is the most accessible proof that you have committed your company to the transition. Waki Box makes that proof measurable, defensible, shareable.
